title: Hypertension overview
text: Hypertension is the medical term for high blood pressure. Long-standing elevation increases risk of heart disease and stroke. First-line management often includes lifestyle changes and medications such as thiazide diuretics, ACE inhibitors, angiotensin receptor blockers, or calcium channel blockers depending on the patient profile.

title: Diabetes basics
text: Diabetes mellitus describes a group of disorders with elevated blood glucose. Type 1 is autoimmune insulin deficiency; type 2 involves insulin resistance. Monitoring glucose, diet, physical activity, and medications such as metformin or insulin are central to care.

title: Influenza
text: Influenza is an acute viral respiratory illness. Vaccination annually reduces severe outcomes. Antiviral therapy may be considered in high-risk patients when started early in the illness.
